The Commission.description: The governing body of the American Mafia, formed in 1931 by Charles "Lucky" Luciano after the Castellammarese War. It consists of the bosses of the Five Families of New York City, the Chicago Outfit, and occasionally other families. The Commission mediates conflicts and oversees Mafia activities in the United States. It has been involved in major events such as the Apalachin meeting and the Mafia Commission Trial.

Al-Nusra Front.description: Sunni Islamist militant group that fought against the Ba'athist regime in the Syrian Civil War, aiming to overthrow Bashar al-Assad and establish an Islamic state. Formed in 2012, it was designated a foreign terrorist organization by the US in 2012 and was the official Syrian affiliate of Al-Qaeda from 2013 to 2016. In 2016 it broke ties with Al-Qaeda and renamed itself Jabhat Fatah Al-Sham, then merged into Hayat Tahrir al-Sham in 2017.

Forty Elephants.description: An all-female London crime syndicate active from the late 19th to mid-20th century, specializing in shoplifting and theft. Based in the Elephant and Castle area, they raided West End stores and masqueraded as housemaids to rob homes. The gang was allied with the Elephant and Castle Mob and known for its longevity and skill in avoiding police detection.
